title: Security binding hardening, fulfillment lifecycle alignment, and audit-driven coverage upgrades
description: Resolved all findings from the acceptance audit for this session: closed a blocker-level nested-resource authorization gap, aligned fulfillment lifecycle contracts across backend/frontend/schema/docs, replaced mirrored unit-test logic with production helper assertions, and added missing negative API tests for object-level binding and evidence validation.

errors.error: Nested compensation approve/reject endpoints accepted mismatched parent ticket IDs (object-level binding gap)
errors.cause: Controller and service resolved compensations by child id only; route parent id was not enforced in lookup/update path.
errors.fix: Passed both `ticketId` and `compensationId` through controller to service, changed lookup to `(id, ticketId)` binding, and returned 404 for mismatched parent-child combinations.

errors.error: Compensation approval tiering risked policy drift due to narrow route gate + role-hardcoded escalation
errors.cause: Approve/reject routes only enforced `compensation:approve-limited`, while controller escalation was role-name based.
errors.fix: Added `requireAnyPermission()` middleware, gated routes with limited/full/override scopes, and resolved approval level from permission matrix (`can(...)`) instead of hardcoded role names.

errors.error: Fulfillment status model inconsistency (draft default and stale lifecycle references)
errors.cause: Schema, service transitions, frontend status unions/views, and docs were not fully synchronized.
errors.fix: Standardized lifecycle to `pending -> processing -> shipped -> delivered/cancelled`, removed `draft` references where applicable, updated schemas/types/views/docs, and kept route/status behavior contract-aligned.

errors.error: Unit tests contained mirrored local business logic patterns (false-confidence risk)
errors.cause: Several suites recreated transition/SLA/cap rules inline instead of validating exported production helpers.
errors.fix: Exported production helper functions/constants from services and refactored tests to call those helpers directly.

errors.error: Negative API coverage gaps for security and file-validation abuse paths
errors.cause: No explicit mismatch tests for ticket/compensation ID tampering and limited evidence-upload rejection coverage.
errors.fix: Added API tests for parent-child mismatch on approve/reject (expect 404), MIME-vs-magic mismatch (422), oversized uploads (413), and duplicate evidence detection (409); normalized Multer errors to deterministic API codes.

errors.error: TypeScript compile regression during containerized rerun (`GLOBAL_CAP` unresolved)
errors.cause: Constant was renamed to `GLOBAL_COMPENSATION_CAP` but one default-rule reference remained stale.
errors.fix: Updated remaining reference to `GLOBAL_COMPENSATION_CAP`, restoring backend/API container builds.

rootCause: Cross-layer drift between security intent, implementation details, test depth, and docs: nested-resource binding assumptions were not enforced in code/tests, and lifecycle contracts were partially synchronized across backend/frontend/schema/documentation.
impact: Before fix, the system risked object-level authorization bypass on compensation endpoints, inconsistent fulfillment behavior across clients/docs, and weaker confidence from unit tests that mirrored logic rather than proving production paths.

validation.method: Dockerized test execution from PowerShell with isolated compose project names and cleanup
